# ++CHRCH Access

*Section: THE BOOK OF TRANSFORMATION: God Momentum*

- id: 22983261
- url: https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/22983261

- [https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/r5nEcldVTSaDCmptjpT6_6DFED5D5-FCE4-46E0-A360-95FC64E09178.jpeg](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/r5nEcldVTSaDCmptjpT6_6DFED5D5-FCE4-46E0-A360-95FC64E09178.jpeg)
- [https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/nbyGoIYSjGUYrqr8O2Yx_IMG_0630.jpeg](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/nbyGoIYSjGUYrqr8O2Yx_IMG_0630.jpeg)
- [https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/mfYMVDNAQNH151OAAbH4_IMG_2346.jpeg](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/mfYMVDNAQNH151OAAbH4_IMG_2346.jpeg)

---

##    20\. Inputs And Outputs 

When people are trying to achieve their goals, most people have the right idea: make a list of things they need to "do". And then go out and do them. 

You look at your todo list every day. And if at the end of the day, most of them are checked off... You know that you did good. 

But sometimes after a few weeks... For some reason... You don't feel like the needle is moving. And so you may be tempted to give up.

It happens every day. All around the world.

And the sad part is... It isn't even your fault.

You were probably never taught the principle of INPUTS and OUTPUTS.

I've used it for my photography, on tour, for writing and even as I built this school.

  


## On the quest for excellence, you eventually learn that all "doing" is not created equal.

  


![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/8D7X9XUKQSqyp7Ym9oOh_thinkerDOER.jpeg)

To have a successful day, you need to see your activities in terms of INPUTS and OUTPUTS.

Some 'doing' is all about taking in information, processing that information, and making the machine stronger.

_Those are the INPUTS._

Then there's the doing that's about creating things, opportunities, or connections with people that weren't there before.

_Those are the OUTPUTS._

OUTPUTS create an energy in the outside world that keeps on creating momentum and magic even when you sleep.

People with high-OUTPUT levels always outperform people with high-INPUT levels.

Even though technically, they're both 'doing'.

## Most people overdose on INPUTS because it's their way of trying to delay potential failure. 

If you're an aspiring writer, yes, it's important to read.

But it's just as important to contact outlets that could publish your work.

If you're a photographer, it's important to organize your photography and workplace.

But it's more important to reach out to new collaborators like brands and models.

Making sure that your OUTPUTS are way higher than your INPUTS is key.

IF...

And only IF... your goal is to make creativity your profession.

If this is only a hobby to you don't worry about INPUTS and OUTPUTS.

But if you want to achieve levels of excellence like Beyonce, Oprah, Kevin Hart, and Drake you're going to have to increase your OUTPUTS.

  


List of INPUTS (important activities that will fool you into thinking you're moving forward, even when you're not):

  


## Thinking

## Organizing

## Researching

## Meditation / Working out

## Creating a new work of art but not sharing it (because it's not ready yet, or because you think it sucks)

  


List of OUTPUTS (important activities that create careers even if you had no INPUTS at all)

  


## Creating AND Sharing a new work of art

## Emailing your list

## Cold calling people that can help you

## Promoting yourself or your work

  


There's no rule to this, but start out with 70% outputs and 30% inputs when you're planning your day (that's an INPUT btw), and from now on and you'll start seeing results.

Don't let the shortness of this module fool you... The impact of this mindset is enormous!

[ Complete and Continue   ]()

"Creating a new work of art but not sharing it (because it's not ready yet, or because you think it sucks)" story of my life. that's insane .. i usually share my art and then, a few days later, i archive it because i think it's not good enough 🫠 as a life coach i'm working on this belief and challenge myself with GM2 to keep all my sharing online but if you have any advice to stop taking one step forward and two steps backward i'm listening ! thank you for this priceless lesson 🙏

Claudis Bridgforth

Awaiting Review 

2 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/22983261/comments/23866494)

This is like secret sauce! I've always hated that feeling of knowing that I'm putting in work... but not getting the results I expected. Such a frustrating experience. It's like you can't see the forest from the trees! In my experience, I would then double down on what I was already doing (ex. more planning ,harder workouts, longer meditations, saving more money for creative classes and workshops) and I would even see some improvements in my creative career as a result, but nothing like the big changes I was expecting. I was smart enough to ask some of my successful creative buddies "what gives? where are my results?" But most answered with some vague version of "Ya just gotta go out and DO IT, man! " But I would think " what is "IT"?!" I am going out and DOING IT with my workouts, planning, and attending creative workshops. Right? I'm so glad that this INPUTS/ OUTPUTS clearly points out "what's hiding in plain sight"

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

This module cracked my brain a little. I never looked at activities through this mindset, im going to flip this around and do the 70 30 rule and see what happens, I will post back here after 7 days + im doing my creative in God mode so this should be really fun and interesting this week! Awesome perspective!

I've started marking (today) what in my to-do list is an "O" (output) and "I" (input). Then I can visually see... Hm, yes, lots of inputs...

wow, mind blown.. its one of these things where I'm like "how did I not see myself doing this!?" I'm very input heavy with my own work, but also I can be the "output guy" who gets stuff done with client work!
